text:
Once upon a summer noon, at the edge of Whispering Woods, an adventurous squirrel named Sally fetched acorns from the oldest oak tree. She scampered up, paused to admire the golden sunshine, then scampered down and gathered a bounty.

Suddenly, she heard a rustling. From behind a fig tree came Mr. Owl. "Good afternoon," he greeted with wisdom-filled eyes.

Sally hopped closer. "Good!" she chuckled. "What are you doing tonight?"

"Just watching the stars." He winked mysteriously.

Suddenly, the stars blazed with a new hue. Mr. Owl stretched. "Time flies. The first full moon is tonight."

Sally blinked. "They come out every month?"

"Exactly!"

She scoured the sky. "They're beautiful!"

"Stay bright," Mr. Owl laughed.

Off he flew. Sally couldn't wait til midnight.

* * *

That very night, the moon dazzled with silver wolves.
